tldr: Matthew, 17 of your 19 live ads use the exact same headline and body word-for-word. Meta sees them as basically one ad, so it quietly shows just one and stops running the rest.

benchmark.context: Most sportswear brands post a fresh ad every 3 weeks. The best ones do it every 2 weeks. You have 19 live ads but really only 4 different ideas, so Meta treats the copies as one ad and shows fewer of them.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: This exact headline and body run word-for-word on more than a dozen of your live ads at once.
Meta sees these copies as one ad and only shows a few of them, so you paid to make ads almost nobody sees.
The opening line 'For those that train with intent' is a slogan, not a reason to click, so people scroll past.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: This is a near-identical twin of another live ad, same words, same button, same page it links to.
You don't say what's on sale or by how much, so 'Ends Soon' has no real pull.
The link goes to your full store instead of the exact products in the video, adding a click between the ad and the sale.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: Yet another copy of the same sale ad, meaning your budget is split across ads Meta will barely show.
There's no price, no discount amount, and no product name, so a shopper has no reason to stop scrolling.
Every one of your video ads sounds the same, so a repeat viewer sees no new hook to pull them back in.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: Same wording as your other sale ads, so this one adds no new angle to test against them.
'Move with precision' and 'show up with purpose' are brand phrases, not something a real customer would search for or care about.
No customer quote, no product name, and no proof this gear actually performs, so the ad rides on vibes alone.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: The button says 'Get offer' but the ad doesn't actually name an offer, so the promise feels empty when they click.
This ad shares its script with a dozen others, so shoppers who saw one already skip this one on sight.
There is no urgency you can actually see, no end date, no percent off, no stock warning, just the words 'Ends Soon'.

ads_compact.headline: BACK IN STOCK.
ads_compact.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.

ads_compact.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads_compact.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.

ads_compact.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads_compact.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.

next_step.urgency: Your top sale ads are on track to stop working within about a week if the same copy keeps running.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Mathan refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Matthew, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads, the pictures, the words, and how long each one has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
